executiveSummary.title: Executive Summary
executiveSummary.content: Doktochain operates at the forefront of healthcare data security, implementing cryptographic audit trails, database-enforced consent mechanisms, and industry-leading encryption standards. This whitepaper details our technical architecture designed to meet and exceed Canadian healthcare regulatory requirements while providing verifiable proof of data integrity and access control.

section1.title: 1. Cryptographic Audit Trails
section1.intro: Every significant action on the Doktochain platform is recorded in an immutable, tamper-evident audit trail powered by cryptographic hashing. This ensures that no data access or modification can occur without permanent, verifiable evidence.

hashChain.title: 1.1 SHA-256 Hash-Chain Architecture
hashChain.intro: Our audit system maintains a hash-chain where each new entry incorporates the cryptographic hash of the previous entry. This creates a mathematically linked sequence where altering any historical record would invalidate all subsequent entries, making tampering immediately detectable.
hashChain.chainFormat: Chain Format: Each audit entry includes: action type, user ID, resource ID, timestamp (UTC), access reason, and previous entry hash
hashChain.immutability: Immutability: Once written, audit records are append-only; no modification or deletion is permitted
hashChain.verification: Verification: Any third party can verify the integrity of the entire chain by recalculating hashes

coverage.title: 1.2 Audit Trail Coverage
coverage.intro: The following actions are permanently recorded in the audit trail:
items: All user authentication events (login, logout, password changes)
Patient health record access (who viewed what, when, and why)
Clinical data modifications (prescriptions written, diagnoses recorded, notes updated)
Consent grants and revocations (with time-based validity windows)
Cross-tenant data access (provider to patient, pharmacy to prescription)
Export and sharing events (PDF downloads, record transfers, FHIR exports)
Administrative actions (user creation, role assignments, policy changes)

patientAccess.title: 1.3 Patient Audit Access
patientAccess.content: Patients maintain continuous, real-time visibility into their audit trail. The platform provides a timestamped log showing exactly who accessed their records, when access occurred, and the reason for access (under which consent grant). This transparency builds trust and provides legal accountability.

section2.title: 2. Bank-Level Encryption
section2.intro: Doktochain employs encryption standards matching those of financial institutions, protecting data both in transit and at rest. This multi-layered approach ensures that even in the unlikely event of a database compromise, personal health information remains unreadable without proper decryption keys.

inTransit.title: 2.1 Encryption in Transit
inTransit.certificate: Certificate: Valid, CA-signed certificates on all endpoints
inTransit.cipherSuites: Cipher Suites: Only modern, cryptographically secure cipher suites (no legacy algorithms)
inTransit.forwardSecrecy: Perfect Forward Secrecy: Session keys are ephemeral; even if long-term keys are compromised, past sessions remain secure
inTransit.hsts: HSTS: HTTP Strict Transport Security enforced to prevent downgrade attacks

atRest.title: 2.2 Encryption at Rest
atRest.keyManagement: Key Management: Keys are stored separately from encrypted data, protected by hardware security modules (HSM) where applicable
atRest.database: Database Encryption: All PostgreSQL tables containing PHI use transparent data encryption (TDE)
atRest.fileStorage: File Storage: Patient documents, images, and attachments stored in encrypted cloud buckets with per-object encryption keys

keyRotation.title: 2.3 Key Rotation Policy
keyRotation.content: Encryption keys are rotated annually or upon key compromise. Old keys are retained in secure, offline storage for a minimum of 7 years to support audit requirements and decryption of historical records.

section3.title: 3. Row-Level Security (RLS) Enforcement
section3.intro: The most powerful aspect of Doktochain's security model is database-enforced Row-Level Security. Unlike application-level access control that can potentially be bypassed through code vulnerabilities, RLS policies are enforced by the database engine itself, making unauthorized access impossible even if application security is compromised.

howItWorks.title: 3.1 How RLS Works
howItWorks.intro: Every query to the database is automatically filtered by RLS policies before results are returned. Users can only see rows they are explicitly authorized to see, based on:
howItWorks.userIdentity: User Identity: The authenticated user's ID, role, and organizational affiliations
howItWorks.ownership: Ownership Rules: Users can access their own records; providers can access patient records only with active consent
howItWorks.consentState: Consent State: Database policies check in real-time whether a valid consent grant exists, including time-based expiration
howItWorks.organizational: Organizational Membership: Clinic staff can access records only for clinics they are affiliated with

consentBased.title: 3.2 Consent-Based Access Control
consentBased.intro: RLS policies actively enforce the Doktochain consent model. Every cross-boundary data access (e.g., a provider viewing a patient's health records) is checked against:
consentBased.existence: Consent Existence: Is there an active, non-expired consent grant from the patient?
consentBased.scope: Scope Verification: Does the consent cover the specific data being accessed?
consentBased.timeWindow: Time Window: Is the consent still within its validity period?
consentBased.revocation: Revocation Status: Has the patient revoked this consent?
consentBased.failure: If any check fails, the database returns zero rows—the data is literally invisible to the requesting user, regardless of their role or privileges.

examples.title: 3.3 RLS Policy Examples
examples.intro: A simplified example of how RLS policies protect patient health records:
examples.patientSelf: Patient Self-Access: SELECT allowed if user_id = auth.uid()
examples.provider: Provider Access: SELECT allowed if patient_id has granted an active, non-expired consent to the provider
examples.pharmacy: Pharmacy Access: SELECT prescription data if consent explicitly grants pharmacy visibility AND prescription is assigned to that pharmacy
examples.admin: Admin Access: Subject to organizational membership (clinic staff access only clinic records, platform admins access all)

section4.title: 4. Zero-Knowledge Architecture Principles
section4.intro: Doktochain applies zero-knowledge principles to sensitive operations, ensuring that no party (including Doktochain staff) can access user data without explicit authorization and audit trail recording.

dataMinimization.title: 4.1 Data Minimization
items: Only health data necessary for direct patient care is collected
No data is retained longer than legally required
Users can request data deletion (subject to retention policies)
Backup data is encrypted and stored in isolated, secure facilities

consentPrimitive.title: 4.2 Consent as a First-Class Primitive
consentPrimitive.intro: Consent is not an afterthought in Doktochain—it is a fundamental, database-enforced primitive. No data access occurs without explicit consent, and every consent grant includes:
items: Clear statement of what data is being shared
Explicit purpose for access (e.g., "treat patient condition", "fill prescription")
Time-bound validity window (expires automatically unless renewed)
Granular scope (e.g., "medication history only", "full health record")
Patient-initiated revocation capability at any time

staffRestrictions.title: 4.3 Staff Data Access Restrictions
staffRestrictions.intro: Even Doktochain staff cannot view patient data. Support access is:
items: Explicitly logged in audit trail
Time-limited (automatically expires after session)
Approved by security team before granting
Visible to the patient in their audit trail

section5.title: 5. Consent Management System
section5.intro: The consent layer is the cornerstone of Doktochain's privacy protection. It balances healthcare interoperability needs with patient privacy through intelligent, time-bounded consent windows.

types.title: 5.1 Consent Types
types.directCare: Direct Care Consent: Patient grants provider access to treat a specific condition; valid for the appointment period plus 30 days for follow-up documentation
types.prescription: Prescription Pharmacy Consent: Patient explicitly directs a prescription to a pharmacy; pharmacy can access only that prescription and related patient demographics
types.healthRecord: Health Record Sharing: Patient grants another provider access to full or partial health history for continuity of care; typically 90 days
types.research: Research/Aggregate Consent: Patient permits de-identified data use for platform analytics (optional, not required for service)

timeBound.title: 5.2 Time-Bound Windows
timeBound.intro: All consent grants include automatic expiration times. After expiration, access is revoked automatically without additional user action. This prevents indefinite data access and encourages periodic re-consent conversations.
timeBound.directCare: Direct care access: Expires 30 days after last appointment interaction
timeBound.prescription: Prescription access: Expires when prescription is marked filled or 60 days, whichever is first
timeBound.healthRecord: Health record sharing: Expires 90 days unless explicitly renewed
timeBound.emergency: Emergency access: Expires after 24 hours (for life-threatening situations)

revocation.title: 5.3 Revocation and Modification
revocation.intro: Patients can revoke any consent at any time through their dashboard. Upon revocation:
items: Access is immediately terminated (within milliseconds, enforced by database RLS)
A revocation event is recorded in the immutable audit trail
The revoking user receives confirmation
The affected provider is notified (to prevent use of outdated cached data)

section6.title: 6. Regulatory Compliance Framework
section6.intro: Doktochain is engineered to comply with all Canadian healthcare privacy regulations and international health data standards.

pipeda.title: 6.1 PIPEDA Compliance (Federal)
pipeda.intro: Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act (PIPEDA) governs private-sector data handling:
pipeda.accountability: Accountability: Designated privacy officer oversees compliance; policies documented and auditable
pipeda.purpose: Identifying Purpose: Users are informed of data collection purposes before providing information
pipeda.consent: Consent: Meaningful consent obtained; users can decline non-essential data collection
pipeda.limiting: Limiting Collection: Only necessary data is collected; no commercial profiling
pipeda.useLimitation: Use Limitation: Data used only for stated purposes or with explicit new consent
pipeda.accuracy: Accuracy: Data kept accurate and up-to-date; users can request corrections
pipeda.safeguards: Safeguards: Appropriate security measures (encryption, access controls, audit trails)
pipeda.individualAccess: Individual Access: Users can request and view their personal information
pipeda.complaints: Complaint Procedures: Clear process for reporting privacy concerns

provincial.title: 6.2 Provincial Health Privacy Laws
provincial.intro: In addition to PIPEDA, Doktochain complies with provincial health-specific legislation:
provincial.ontario: Ontario (PHIPA): Personal Health Information Protection Act — governs health information handling by healthcare providers and facilities
provincial.alberta: Alberta (HIA): Health Information Act — provides privacy protection for personal health information
provincial.bc: British Columbia (PIPA): Personal Information Protection Act — applies to private-sector health organizations
provincial.quebec: Quebec (Law 25): Updated privacy law with stronger consent and security requirements
provincial.other: Manitoba, Saskatchewan, Nova Scotia: Similar provincial privacy legislation applicable in respective jurisdictions

hipaa.title: 6.3 HIPAA Alignment (U.S. Reciprocity)
hipaa.intro: Though not required for Canadian operations, Doktochain exceeds HIPAA standards to support cross-border healthcare partnerships:
items: Privacy Rule: Minimum necessary standard enforced
Security Rule: Technical safeguards exceed requirements (AES-256 vs. required encryption)
Breach Notification: Mandatory 60-day notification for any unauthorized access

fhir.title: 6.4 FHIR R4 Compliance
fhir.intro: Doktochain's clinical data layer is designed around FHIR (Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources) R4 standards, enabling seamless data exchange with external EHRs and provincial systems:
fhir.resources: Supported Resources: Patient, Observation, Condition, Procedure, MedicationRequest, Immunization, AllergyIntolerance, Encounter, DiagnosticReport
fhir.coding: Coding Systems: ICD-10 (diagnoses/conditions), SNOMED CT (clinical concepts), CPT (procedures), LOINC (lab results), DIN (medications)
fhir.validation: Validation: All clinical data validated against FHIR schema before storage
fhir.export: Export: Patients can export their complete health record in standard FHIR bundles

section7.title: 7. Data Residency and Infrastructure Security
section7.intro: All patient data is stored and processed within Canada on dedicated, privately managed cloud infrastructure. This architecture provides full control over network isolation, encryption key management, and access policies while complying with Canadian data residency requirements.

hosting.title: 7.1 Canadian Data Hosting
hosting.primary: Primary Region: Montreal, Quebec (ca-central-1) — all compute, database, and storage resources deployed exclusively in Canadian data centres
hosting.dr: Disaster Recovery Region: Calgary, Alberta (ca-west-1) — cross-region replication for business continuity
hosting.geographic: Geographic Restriction: Data never leaves Canadian jurisdiction without explicit user consent; infrastructure policies enforce region-locked deployment
hosting.iac: Infrastructure as Code: All cloud resources are defined declaratively, enabling reproducible deployments and auditable infrastructure changes

network.title: 7.2 Network Security and Isolation
network.intro: The platform operates within a private virtual network architecture designed to minimize attack surface and prevent unauthorized access to sensitive systems.
network.private: Private Network Architecture: Database and compute resources run in private subnets with no direct internet access and no public IP addresses
network.outbound: Outbound-Only Internet Access: Private resources reach external services (e.g., payment processors) through a network address translation gateway — no inbound connections are possible
network.pooling: Connection Pooling Proxy: A managed proxy layer sits between compute functions and the database, enforcing connection limits, TLS encryption, and credential rotation without exposing the database endpoint
network.entry: Single Public Entry Point: All API traffic enters through a managed API gateway that validates authentication tokens before any request reaches backend services
network.cdn: Content Delivery Network: Static assets and frontend resources are served through a global CDN with web application firewall (WAF) rules for DDoS protection and request filtering
network.securityGroups: Security Groups: Granular firewall rules restrict database access to only authorized compute functions — no other services or users can initiate connections

keyManagement.title: 7.3 Key Management and Encryption Infrastructure
keyManagement.intro: Encryption keys are managed through a dedicated, hardware-backed key management service that enforces separation of duties between key administrators and data users.
keyManagement.hsm: Hardware Security Modules: All master encryption keys are generated and stored in FIPS 140-2 Level 3 validated hardware security modules
keyManagement.database: Database Encryption: The managed PostgreSQL database uses transparent data encryption with dedicated, automatically rotated keys
keyManagement.fileStorage: File Storage Encryption: Patient documents and attachments use server-side encryption with per-object keys derived from master keys
keyManagement.secrets: Secrets Management: Third-party credentials (payment processor keys, video consultation tokens) are stored in a managed secrets vault with automatic rotation support
keyManagement.rotation: Key Rotation: Master keys are rotated annually; data keys are rotated on every write operation; old keys are retained securely for historical decryption

monitoring.title: 7.4 Audit and Monitoring Infrastructure
monitoring.intro: Beyond the application-level cryptographic audit trail (Section 1), the infrastructure itself maintains comprehensive logging and monitoring at every layer.
monitoring.apiLogging: API Activity Logging: Every cloud API call across the account is logged with caller identity, timestamp, source IP, and request parameters — retained for 90 days minimum
monitoring.realtime: Real-Time Monitoring: Custom dashboards and automated alarms monitor database performance, API error rates, authentication failures, and resource utilization
monitoring.flowLogs: Network Flow Logs: All network traffic within the private virtual network is logged for forensic analysis and anomaly detection
monitoring.ledger: Immutable Ledger Enhancement: As an additional layer of assurance, critical audit events can be replicated to a purpose-built immutable ledger database that provides cryptographic verification of data integrity, complementing the application-level hash-chain audit trail
monitoring.alerting: Alerting: Automated alerts notify the security team of suspicious patterns including repeated authentication failures, unusual data access volumes, and infrastructure configuration changes

disaster.title: 7.5 Disaster Recovery and Business Continuity
disaster.rpo: RPO (Recovery Point Objective): 1 hour — maximum data loss if disaster occurs
disaster.rto: RTO (Recovery Time Objective): 15 minutes — platform available again after failover
disaster.backup: Continuous Backup: The managed PostgreSQL database performs continuous backups with point-in-time recovery to any second within the retention window
disaster.multiAz: Multi-AZ Redundancy: Database and compute resources are deployed across multiple availability zones within the primary region; automatic failover occurs if a zone becomes unavailable
disaster.crossRegion: Cross-Region Replication: Critical data is replicated to the disaster recovery region for geographic redundancy
disaster.reproducibility: Infrastructure Reproducibility: All resources are defined as code, enabling full environment reconstruction in a new region within hours if catastrophic regional failure occurs
disaster.testing: Testing: Disaster recovery drills conducted quarterly; failover procedures tested and documented

soc2.title: 7.6 SOC 2 Type II Certification
soc2.intro: Doktochain undergoes annual SOC 2 Type II audits by independent third-party auditors, validating:
items: Security controls are designed and operating effectively
Availability and processing integrity of systems
Confidentiality of patient information
Privacy controls comply with PIPEDA and provincial standards

section8.title: 8. Interoperability and Cross-System Trust
section8.intro: One of Doktochain's strengths is enabling secure health data sharing with external systems while maintaining complete audit accountability.

fhirGateway.title: 8.1 FHIR Gateway Architecture
fhirGateway.intro: Doktochain operates a FHIR gateway that allows external EHRs and provincial health systems to request patient records. All requests go through the same security pipeline:
items: Request includes patient ID and the requesting system's OAuth credentials
Gateway checks if patient has granted consent to the requesting system
If consent is active and time-valid, patient's FHIR bundle is returned (encrypted)
Request is logged to immutable audit trail with system identity and timestamp
Patient sees the request in their audit trail immediately

provincial.title: 8.2 Provincial EHR Connections (COMING SOON ...)
provincial.intro: Doktochain integrates with provincial health information exchanges where available:
provincial.bc: British Columbia: Health Information Exchange (HIE) pilot connections
provincial.note: These integrations are read-only; patient data is never pushed without explicit consent, and every read is audited.

prescription.title: 8.3 Prescription Workflow Auditing
prescription.intro: The prescription pipeline illustrates complete audit coverage:
items: Provider writes prescription → audit entry created with provider identity and reason
Patient receives notification → receipt logged
Patient directs to specific pharmacy → redirection decision logged with timestamp
Pharmacy receives prescription → access logged with pharmacy identity
Pharmacy fills or declines → fulfillment decision logged
Patient views filled status → access logged
All actions recorded to immutable hash-chain; any party can independently verify integrity

penetration.title: 9.1 Penetration Testing
penetration.intro: Doktochain undergoes quarterly penetration testing by independent security firms. Tests cover:
items: Network layer attacks (port scanning, protocol exploits)
Application layer attacks (SQL injection, XSS, CSRF)
Authentication/authorization bypass attempts
Encryption algorithm and implementation weaknesses
RLS policy bypass attempts
Audit trail tampering scenarios

codeReview.title: 9.2 Security Code Review
codeReview.content: All code changes undergo security-focused review before deployment. Automated scanning detects known vulnerabilities; senior engineers verify no novel security issues are introduced.

incident.title: 9.3 Incident Response Plan
incident.intro: In the event of a security incident:
incident.detection: Detection: 24/7 monitoring of logs, intrusion detection systems, and audit alerts
incident.response: Response: Incident response team activated within 15 minutes; affected systems isolated
incident.investigation: Investigation: Forensic analysis using immutable audit trail; determine scope and cause
incident.notification: Notification: Affected users and regulators notified within 72 hours (per PIPEDA)
incident.remediation: Remediation: Fixes deployed; security improvements implemented to prevent recurrence

rows.details: Private subnets; no public database endpoints
Full API call logging; optional cryptographic ledger

regulatory.title: 10.2 Regulatory References
items: PIPEDA (Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act) — S.C. 1998, c. 5
PHIPA (Ontario Personal Health Information Protection Act) — S.O. 2004, c. 3
HIA (Alberta Health Information Act) — RSA 2000, c. H-5
PIPA (BC Personal Information Protection Act) — S.B.C. 2003, c. 63
FHIR R4 — https://www.hl7.org/fhir/R4/
SOC 2 Type II — AICPA Trust Services Criteria

commitment.title: Commitment to Transparency
commitment.content: Doktochain's security model is designed for transparency and patient empowerment. Patients can view their complete audit trail, revoke consent at any time, and independently verify data integrity. We believe that trust in healthcare technology is built not through security theater, but through verifiable, auditable systems. This whitepaper is available to healthcare institutions, regulators, and patients for evaluation and independent verification.

privacy.intro1: Doktochain Inc. ("Doktochain", "we", "us", or "our") is committed to protecting the privacy and security of personal information, including personal health information (PHI), collected through our platform. This Privacy Policy describes how we collect, use, disclose, and safeguard your information when you use the Doktochain platform, website, and related services.
privacy.intro2: We operate in compliance with the Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act (PIPEDA) and applicable provincial health privacy legislation, including Ontario's Personal Health Information Protection Act (PHIPA), Alberta's Health Information Act (HIA), and British Columbia's Personal Information Protection Act (PIPA).

personal.title: 2.1 Personal Information
personal.intro: We may collect the following types of personal information:
items: Full name, date of birth, gender, and contact information (email, phone, address)
Government-issued identification numbers (health card numbers) for insurance verification
Account credentials and authentication data
Payment and billing information
Professional credentials and licensing information (for healthcare providers)

health.title: 2.2 Personal Health Information
health.intro: When you use our healthcare services, we may collect:
items: Medical history, diagnoses, and treatment records
Prescription and medication information
Lab results, imaging reports, and clinical notes
Allergy and immunization records
Vital signs and health tracking data
Telemedicine consultation recordings (with consent)

technical.title: 2.3 Technical Information
items: Device type, operating system, and browser information
IP address and approximate location data
Usage patterns and interaction data within the platform
Cookies and similar tracking technologies

section3.title: 3. How We Use Your Information
section3.intro: We use the information we collect to:
items: Provide, maintain, and improve our healthcare platform services
Facilitate appointments, consultations, and prescription management
Process payments and manage billing
Communicate with you about appointments, prescriptions, and account matters
Ensure the security and integrity of our platform
Comply with legal and regulatory obligations
Generate anonymized analytics to improve healthcare outcomes

section4.title: 4. Consent
section4.intro: Doktochain employs a database-enforced consent model. Before any healthcare provider can access your health information, you must provide explicit consent through our platform. Key features of our consent model include:
items: Granular consent: You control which providers can access specific categories of your health data
Time-based access windows: Consent grants are limited to specific time periods relevant to your care
Revocable at any time: You may withdraw consent at any time through your patient dashboard
Audit trail: Every consent action is recorded in our tamper-evident hash-chain audit system

section5.title: 5. How We Share Your Information
section5.intro: We may share your information only in the following circumstances:
items: With your healthcare providers: Only with your explicit consent and within approved time windows
With pharmacies: When you direct a prescription to a pharmacy of your choice
With payment processors: To process payments securely through Stripe
As required by law: When compelled by legal process, regulation, or to protect safety
With your express authorization: For any other purposes you specifically approve
section5.noSale: We never sell your personal information or personal health information to third parties.

section6.title: 6. Data Security
section6.intro: We implement comprehensive security measures including:
items: End-to-end encryption for data in transit and at rest
Row-Level Security (RLS) enforced at the database level
Cryptographic hash-chain audit trail for all data access events
Multi-factor authentication support
Regular security assessments and penetration testing
FHIR R4-compliant data structures for healthcare interoperability

section7.title: 7. Data Retention
section7.content: We retain personal health information in accordance with applicable Canadian healthcare legislation. Medical records are retained for a minimum of 10 years from the last date of service, or longer as required by provincial regulations. You may request deletion of non-clinical personal information by contacting our privacy office.

section8.title: 8. Your Rights
section8.intro: Under PIPEDA and applicable provincial legislation, you have the right to:
items: Access your personal information and health records at no cost
Request corrections to inaccurate information
Withdraw consent for data collection and use
File a complaint with our Privacy Officer or the Office of the Privacy Commissioner of Canada
Export your health data in FHIR-compatible formats

section9.title: 9. Children's Privacy
section9.content: Our platform supports family accounts where parents or legal guardians can manage health information for minor dependents. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children under 13 without verifiable parental consent.

section10.title: 10. Contact Us
section10.intro: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy or wish to exercise your privacy rights, please contact our Privacy Officer:
items: Email: privacy@doktochain.ca
Phone: 1-800-DOKTO-CHAIN
Address: Toronto, ON, Canada

section1.title: 1. Acceptance of Terms
section1.content: By accessing or using the Doktochain platform ("Platform"), you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service ("Terms"). If you do not agree to these Terms, you may not access or use the Platform. These Terms constitute a legally binding agreement between you and Doktochain Inc. ("Doktochain", "we", "us", or "our").

section2.title: 2. Description of Services
section2.intro: Doktochain is a healthcare technology platform that connects patients with healthcare providers and pharmacies across Canada. Our services include:
items: Provider discovery and appointment booking
Video and in-person consultation facilitation
Electronic prescription management and pharmacy fulfillment
Health records management and secure data sharing
Payment processing for healthcare services
Practice management tools for healthcare providers
section2.disclaimer: Important: Doktochain is a technology platform and does not provide medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. All clinical decisions are made independently by licensed healthcare providers.

registration.title: 3.1 Registration
registration.content: To use certain features of the Platform, you must create an account. You agree to provide accurate, current, and complete information during registration and to keep your account information updated.

accountTypes.title: 3.2 Account Types
items: Patient accounts: For individuals seeking healthcare services
Provider accounts: For licensed healthcare professionals (subject to credential verification)
Pharmacy accounts: For licensed pharmacies (subject to licensing verification)
Clinic accounts: For healthcare organizations managing multiple providers

security.title: 3.3 Account Security
security.content: You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials. You must immediately notify us of any unauthorized use of your account. We are not liable for any loss arising from unauthorized use of your account.

section4.title: 4. Healthcare Provider Terms
section4.intro: Healthcare providers using the Platform agree to:
items: Maintain valid and current professional licenses in their practicing province(s)
Provide accurate credential and qualification information
Comply with all applicable medical regulations and standards of care
Maintain appropriate professional liability insurance
Use the Platform's consent system before accessing patient data
Document clinical encounters in accordance with regulatory requirements

section5.title: 5. Pharmacy Terms
section5.intro: Pharmacies using the Platform agree to:
items: Maintain valid pharmacy licenses in their operating province(s)
Dispense medications in accordance with applicable pharmacy regulations
Maintain accurate inventory records
Process prescriptions received through the Platform in a timely manner
Comply with all drug safety and reporting obligations

section6.title: 6. Patient Terms
section6.intro: Patients using the Platform agree to:
items: Provide accurate personal and health information
Understand that telemedicine has limitations compared to in-person visits
Seek emergency care by calling 911 for emergencies, not through the Platform
Make timely payment for services rendered
Manage consent grants responsibly

patientPayments.title: 7.1 Patient Payments
patientPayments.content: Patients are responsible for payment of consultation fees, prescription costs, and any other charges incurred through the Platform. Payments are processed securely through Stripe.

subscriptions.title: 7.2 Provider and Pharmacy Subscriptions
subscriptions.content: Providers and pharmacies may be subject to subscription fees as outlined in their selected plan. Transaction fees may apply to certain payment types as specified in the pricing schedule.

refunds.title: 7.3 Refunds
refunds.content: Refund policies vary by service type. Cancelled appointments may be subject to cancellation fees as specified by the provider. Disputed charges should be reported within 30 days.

section8.title: 8. Intellectual Property
section8.content: The Platform, including its design, features, content, and underlying technology, is owned by Doktochain and is protected by Canadian and international intellectual property laws. Users retain ownership of their personal data and health records.

section9.title: 9. Prohibited Conduct
items: Use the Platform for any unlawful purpose
Attempt to access another user's account or data without authorization
Interfere with or disrupt the Platform's operation
Upload malicious software or content
Scrape, crawl, or otherwise extract data from the Platform
Impersonate any person or entity
Use the Platform to distribute unsolicited communications

section10.title: 10. Limitation of Liability
section10.para1: To the maximum extent permitted by law, Doktochain shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages arising from your use of the Platform. Our total liability shall not exceed the fees you paid to us in the 12 months preceding the claim.
section10.para2: Doktochain is not responsible for the quality of care provided by healthcare providers or the accuracy of information provided by pharmacies on the Platform.

section11.title: 11. Termination
section11.content: Either party may terminate the relationship at any time. We may suspend or terminate your account if you violate these Terms. Upon termination, your right to use the Platform ceases, but provisions regarding data retention, intellectual property, and limitation of liability shall survive.

section12.title: 12. Governing Law
section12.content: These Terms are governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Province of Ontario and the federal laws of Canada applicable therein. Any disputes shall be resolved in the courts of Ontario, Canada.

section13.title: 13. Changes to These Terms
section13.content: We may update these Terms from time to time. We will notify you of material changes through the Platform or via email. Your continued use of the Platform after changes become effective constitutes acceptance of the updated Terms.

section14.title: 14. Contact
section14.intro: For questions about these Terms, please contact us:
items: Email: legal@doktochain.ca
Phone: 1-800-DOKTO-CHAIN
Address: Toronto, ON, Canada

section1.title: 1. What Are Cookies
section1.content: Cookies are small text files stored on your device when you visit a website. They help the website remember your preferences and understand how you interact with it. Doktochain uses cookies and similar technologies to provide, secure, and improve our platform.

section2.title: 2. How We Use Cookies
section2.intro: We use cookies for the following purposes:

essential.title: 2.1 Essential Cookies
essential.intro: These cookies are necessary for the Platform to function and cannot be disabled. They include:
items: Authentication cookies: Keep you logged in securely during your session
Security cookies: Protect against cross-site request forgery and other threats
Session management: Maintain your session state as you navigate the Platform
Consent preferences: Remember your cookie consent choices

functional.title: 2.2 Functional Cookies
functional.intro: These cookies enable enhanced functionality and personalization:
items: Language preferences: Remember your preferred language settings
Theme preferences: Store your display preferences (light/dark mode)
Recent searches: Remember your recent provider or specialty searches

analytics.title: 2.3 Analytics Cookies
analytics.intro: These cookies help us understand how users interact with the Platform so we can improve it:
items: Page view and navigation patterns
Feature usage statistics
Error and performance monitoring
analytics.note: Analytics data is aggregated and anonymized. We do not use analytics cookies to track individual health information or clinical interactions.

section3.title: 3. Third-Party Cookies
section3.intro: Some third-party services used by Doktochain may set their own cookies:
items: Supabase: Authentication and session management
Stripe: Payment processing and fraud prevention
Daily.co: Video consultation functionality
section3.note: These third parties have their own privacy and cookie policies. We encourage you to review their policies for more information.

section4.title: 4. Managing Cookies
section4.intro: You can manage cookies through several methods:

browser.title: 4.1 Browser Settings
browser.content: Most web browsers allow you to control cookies through their settings. You can typically find these under "Privacy" or "Security" in your browser preferences. Note that disabling essential cookies may prevent you from using certain Platform features.

controls.title: 4.2 Specific Cookie Controls
items: Essential cookies: Cannot be disabled as they are required for Platform operation
Functional cookies: Can be disabled, but some features may not work as expected
Analytics cookies: Can be opted out of without affecting Platform functionality

section5.title: 5. Data Protection
section5.content: Cookie data is handled in accordance with our Privacy Policy and PIPEDA requirements. We do not store personal health information in cookies. Authentication tokens stored in cookies are encrypted and expire after the session ends.

section6.title: 6. Updates to This Policy
section6.content: We may update this Cookie Policy as our technology or legal requirements change. We will notify you of significant changes through the Platform.

section7.title: 7. Contact
section7.intro: For questions about our use of cookies, please contact:
items: Email: privacy@doktochain.ca
Phone: 1-800-DOKTO-CHAIN

section1.title: 1. Overview
section1.content: While Doktochain is a Canadian healthcare platform primarily governed by PIPEDA and provincial health privacy legislation, we recognize that healthcare data protection standards are international in scope. We design our security and privacy practices to meet or exceed the standards set by both Canadian legislation and the United States Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA).

section2.title: 2. Canadian Privacy Framework
items: PIPEDA -- Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act (federal)
PHIPA -- Personal Health Information Protection Act (Ontario)
HIA -- Health Information Act (Alberta)
PIPA -- Personal Information Protection Act (British Columbia)
Act Respecting the Protection of Personal Information in the Private Sector (Quebec)
section2.note: These laws collectively provide comprehensive protection for personal health information that is comparable to, and in many respects exceeds, HIPAA requirements.

section3.title: 3. Security Safeguards
section3.intro: Doktochain implements administrative, technical, and physical safeguards that align with both Canadian requirements and HIPAA's Security Rule:

administrative.title: 3.1 Administrative Safeguards
items: Designated Privacy Officer responsible for compliance oversight
Regular risk assessments and security audits
Workforce training on privacy and security practices
Incident response and breach notification procedures
Business Associate Agreements with all service providers handling PHI

technical.title: 3.2 Technical Safeguards
items: Access controls: Role-based access with Row-Level Security (RLS) enforced at the database layer
Encryption: AES-256 encryption at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit
Audit logging: Cryptographic hash-chain audit trail for all data access events
Authentication: Secure authentication with multi-factor authentication support
Consent enforcement: Database-level consent checks with time-based access windows
Data integrity: FHIR R4-compliant data structures with referential integrity constraints

physical.title: 3.3 Physical Safeguards
items: Data hosted on SOC 2 Type II certified infrastructure
Canadian data residency -- all health data stored within Canada
Redundant backups with geographic distribution within Canadian borders
Environmental controls and physical access restrictions at data centers

section4.title: 4. Consent-Based Access Model
section4.intro: Doktochain's consent model goes beyond minimum regulatory requirements by implementing database-enforced consent that cannot be bypassed by application-level code:
items: Every cross-provider data access requires active patient consent
Consent grants include specific time windows and data categories
All consent actions are recorded in the tamper-evident audit trail
Patients can view, modify, and revoke consent at any time
Expired consent windows automatically terminate access

section5.title: 5. Breach Notification
section5.intro: In the event of a data breach involving personal health information, Doktochain will:
items: Notify affected individuals as soon as feasible, and no later than required by applicable law
Report to the Office of the Privacy Commissioner of Canada and relevant provincial regulators
Document the breach, its impact, and remediation steps taken
Implement measures to prevent recurrence

section6.title: 6. Patient Rights
section6.intro: Consistent with both Canadian privacy law and HIPAA's Privacy Rule, Doktochain ensures patients can:
items: Access their complete health records at no charge through the patient dashboard
Request amendments to their records
Receive an accounting of disclosures via the audit trail
Export their data in FHIR-compatible formats
File complaints with the Privacy Officer or relevant regulatory bodies

section7.title: 7. Continuous Improvement
section7.intro: We continuously evaluate and enhance our security posture through:
items: Regular penetration testing and vulnerability assessments
Annual compliance reviews against evolving regulatory standards
Ongoing monitoring of security threats and industry best practices
Staff training and awareness programs

section8.title: 8. Contact
section8.intro: For questions about our compliance practices or to report a security concern:
items: Privacy Officer: privacy@doktochain.ca
Security Team: security@doktochain.ca
Phone: 1-800-DOKTO-CHAIN
section8.moreInfo: For more information about how we protect your data, please review our

section1.title: 1. Our Commitment
section1.para1: Doktochain is committed to ensuring that our platform is accessible to all users, including people with disabilities. We believe that everyone deserves equal access to healthcare services, and we strive to make our digital experience as inclusive as possible.
section1.para2: We are committed to meeting or exceeding the requirements of the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act (AODA) and the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.1 Level AA.

section2.title: 2. Accessibility Standards
section2.intro: Our platform is designed and developed to conform with:
items: WCAG 2.1 Level AA: The internationally recognized standard for web accessibility
AODA: Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act, Integrated Accessibility Standards Regulation
ACA: Accessible Canada Act requirements for federally regulated entities

visual.title: 3.1 Visual Accessibility
items: Sufficient color contrast ratios throughout the interface
Resizable text that can be scaled up to 200% without loss of content
Alternative text for all meaningful images and icons
Clear visual focus indicators for keyboard navigation
No content that relies solely on color to convey information

navigation.title: 3.2 Navigation and Interaction
items: Full keyboard navigability for all Platform features
Logical tab order and focus management
Skip navigation links for quick access to main content
Consistent navigation patterns across all pages
Clear and descriptive link text

content.title: 3.3 Content Accessibility
items: Clear heading hierarchy for screen reader navigation
Descriptive labels for all form fields
Error messages that clearly identify and describe issues
Sufficient time provided for time-sensitive actions
Content structured with semantic HTML elements

assistive.title: 3.4 Assistive Technology Support
items: ARIA landmarks and roles for screen reader compatibility
Live regions for dynamic content updates
Compatible with popular screen readers (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver)
Support for browser zoom and text-only zoom

section4.title: 4. Telemedicine Accessibility
section4.intro: Our video consultation features are designed with accessibility in mind:
items: Keyboard-accessible video controls
Secure messaging as an alternative communication channel
Clear audio indicators for connection status
Compatibility with assistive devices

section5.title: 5. Ongoing Efforts
section5.intro: We are continuously working to improve accessibility through:
items: Regular accessibility audits by qualified assessors
Automated and manual testing during development
User feedback incorporation from people with disabilities
Staff training on accessibility best practices
Monitoring of accessibility standards updates

section6.title: 6. Known Limitations
section6.content: While we strive for full accessibility, some third-party content or features may have limitations beyond our direct control. We work with our technology partners to advocate for improved accessibility in their services, including video conferencing and payment processing components.

section7.title: 7. Feedback and Assistance
section7.intro: We welcome your feedback on the accessibility of the Doktochain platform. If you encounter any accessibility barriers or need assistance, please contact us:
items: Email: accessibility@doktochain.ca
Phone: 1-800-DOKTO-CHAIN
Address: Toronto, ON, Canada
section7.response: We aim to respond to accessibility feedback within 2 business days and to resolve reported barriers as quickly as possible.

section8.title: 8. Formal Complaints
section8.content: If you are not satisfied with our response to an accessibility concern, you may file a formal complaint with the Accessibility Directorate of Ontario or the relevant provincial accessibility body.

section1.title: 1. Overview
section1.content: Doktochain Inc. ("Doktochain", "we", "us", or "our") is committed to fair and transparent refund practices for all services offered through our platform. This Refund Policy outlines the circumstances under which full or partial refunds may be granted for consultation fees, subscription plans, and pharmacy orders. By using the Doktochain platform, you agree to the terms of this Refund Policy.

section2.title: 2. Refund Eligibility by Service Type
section2.intro: Refund eligibility varies depending on the type of service. The following sections describe the specific refund terms for each service category offered through the Doktochain platform.

consultations.title: 2.1 Medical Consultations (In-Person and Telemedicine)
consultations.intro: Refund eligibility for medical consultations depends on when the cancellation occurs and whether the consultation was completed:
items: Cancellation more than 24 hours before the appointment: Full refund of the consultation fee
Cancellation between 4 and 24 hours before the appointment: Partial refund of 50% of the consultation fee
Cancellation less than 4 hours before the appointment or no-show: No refund; the full consultation fee is retained
Technical failure (platform or provider side): Full refund if the consultation could not take place due to platform-related technical issues, verified by our support team
Provider cancellation: Full refund issued automatically if the healthcare provider cancels the appointment

subscriptions.title: 2.2 Subscription Plans (Providers, Pharmacies, and Clinics)
subscriptions.intro: Subscription fees for healthcare providers, pharmacies, and clinics are subject to the following refund terms:
items: Free trial period: No charge during the trial period. You will not be billed if you cancel before the trial expires
Cancellation within 14 days of first paid billing: Full refund of the current billing period, provided no significant platform usage has occurred (e.g., fewer than 5 patient interactions)
Cancellation after 14 days of paid billing: No refund for the current billing period. Your subscription will remain active until the end of the current billing cycle
Annual subscription cancellation: Partial refund calculated on a pro-rata basis for unused full months remaining in the annual term, minus a 10% early termination fee
Downgrade: No refund for the current period. The lower-tier pricing takes effect at the start of the next billing cycle

pharmacy.title: 2.3 Pharmacy Orders and Prescriptions
pharmacy.intro: Refunds for pharmacy orders are subject to health and safety regulations:
items: Order cancelled before dispensing: Full refund if the pharmacy has not yet begun preparing the order
Order cancelled after dispensing: No refund, as dispensed medications cannot be restocked per Canadian pharmacy regulations
Incorrect or damaged order: Full refund or replacement for orders that arrive damaged, contain the wrong medication, or have incorrect quantities. You must report the issue within 48 hours of receipt
OTC products (over-the-counter): Full refund within 14 days of delivery if the product is unopened and in its original packaging. Opened OTC products are not eligible for refund

section3.title: 3. Full vs. Partial Refund Breakdown
section3.intro: The following summarizes the conditions for full and partial refunds across all service categories.

full.title: 3.1 Full Refund Conditions
full.intro: A full refund (100% of the amount paid) is issued under the following circumstances:
items: Consultation cancelled more than 24 hours in advance
Provider cancels the appointment for any reason
Platform technical failure that prevents the consultation from occurring
Subscription cancelled within 14 days of first paid billing (with minimal usage)
Pharmacy order cancelled before dispensing has begun
Incorrect or damaged pharmacy order reported within 48 hours
Duplicate charge or billing error confirmed by our billing team

partial.title: 3.2 Partial Refund Conditions
partial.intro: A partial refund is issued when the service was partially delivered or the cancellation falls within a late-notice window:
items: 50% refund: Consultation cancelled between 4 and 24 hours before the scheduled time
Pro-rata refund (minus 10% fee): Annual subscription cancelled after 14 days, calculated for unused full months remaining
Case-by-case: Consultations where significant technical issues occurred partway through the session, assessed by our support team
Partial delivery: Pharmacy orders where only a portion of the items were incorrect or damaged

nonRefundable.title: 3.3 Non-Refundable Charges
nonRefundable.intro: The following charges are not eligible for any refund:
items: Consultations that were completed in full
No-show appointments (patient did not attend without prior cancellation)
Cancellations made less than 4 hours before the scheduled appointment
Dispensed prescription medications (per Canadian pharmacy regulations)
Opened over-the-counter products
Monthly subscription fees after the 14-day refund window has passed
Payment processing fees charged by third-party providers (Stripe)

section4.title: 4. How to Request a Refund
section4.intro: To request a refund, follow these steps:
items: Step 1: Log in to your Doktochain account and navigate to your Billing or Appointments section
Step 2: Locate the transaction or appointment for which you are requesting a refund
Step 3: Click "Request Refund" and select the reason for your request
Step 4: Provide any supporting details or documentation (e.g., screenshots of technical issues)
Step 5: Submit your request. You will receive a confirmation email with a reference number
Alternatively, you may contact our support team directly at billing@doktochain.ca or call 1-800-DOKTO-CHAIN

section5.title: 5. Refund Processing Timeline
section5.intro: Once a refund is approved, the following timelines apply:
items: Credit/debit card refunds: 5 to 10 business days to appear on your statement, depending on your bank
Provider cancellation refunds: Processed automatically within 1 business day
Subscription refunds: Processed within 3 to 5 business days after approval
Pharmacy order refunds: Processed within 5 to 7 business days after the return or issue is verified
All refunds are issued to the original payment method used for the transaction

section6.title: 6. Dispute Resolution
section6.intro: If you disagree with a refund decision, you have the following options:
items: Internal review: Request a review of the decision by contacting our billing team at billing@doktochain.ca within 30 days of the original decision
Escalation: If the internal review does not resolve the issue, you may escalate to our Customer Experience Manager
Chargeback: You retain the right to dispute the charge with your credit card provider, though we encourage resolving disputes directly with us first to avoid delays
Consumer protection: You may also contact the applicable provincial consumer protection agency for additional guidance

section7.title: 7. Policy Updates
section7.content: Doktochain reserves the right to update this Refund Policy at any time. Material changes will be communicated through the platform and via email to registered users. Your continued use of the platform after changes take effect constitutes acceptance of the updated policy. The most current version of this Refund Policy is always available on our website.

section8.title: 8. Contact Us
section8.intro: For questions about this Refund Policy or to request a refund, please contact us:
items: Email: billing@doktochain.ca
Phone: 1-800-DOKTO-CHAIN
Address: Toronto, ON, Canada
